narrative:
Cristian Romero is a pivotal Centre-Back for Argentina. His overall rating of 7.22/10 places him among the top 4% of centre-backs at this tournament. This rating is influenced by 65% from his club form over the last two seasons and 35% from his performances at this World Cup.

Romero's defensive impact is clear from his World Cup statistics, where he averages 1.751 tackles and 1.751 interceptions per 90 minutes. He also contributes significantly with 2.802 clearances per 90 minutes. His attacking involvement is limited, reflected by 0.042 expected goals and 0.018 expected assists per 90 minutes.

Romero anchors Argentina's defence, providing crucial stability and winning key duels across his 257 minutes played. Scouts and fans should continue to watch his disciplined defensive positioning and ability to consistently break up opposition attacks. His robust presence will be essential for his national team's aspirations in the remaining matches.
